sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Stabilis Solutions cut to Hold at Maxim based on LNG timeline update
feed.summary: Maxim has downgraded Stabilis Solutions (SLNG) from Buy to Hold, citing a longer estimated timeline for natural gas use as engine fuel, higher capital requirements for LNG delivery infrastructure, and limited barriers to entry. This downgrade is accompanied by reduced revenue and EBITDA estimates due to projections of fewer gallons of liquefied natural gas delivered and lower gross margins per gallon. The analyst also notes Stabilis Solutions CEO's previous statement that 2027 is "shaping up to be a historic year" for the company.

feed.title: Auddia Reports Discovr Radio MVP Exceeds User Engagement Projections in First 30 Days
feed.summary: Auddia Inc. announced that its Discovr Radio MVP has surpassed initial user engagement projections within its first 30 days. The platform, designed to enhance radio listening, showed higher-than-expected app downloads and active usage, along with positive user feedback. This milestone is crucial for Auddia as it continues to develop the product based on early user insights.

feed.title: AGTC Stock Price, News & Analysis
feed.summary: Applied Genetic Technologies Corporation (AGTC) was a clinical-stage biotechnology company focused on developing gene therapies for rare ophthalmic, otologic, and central nervous system diseases. The company was acquired by Syncona Limited in December 2022, after which its stock was delisted from Nasdaq and it became a privately held subsidiary. AGTC's historical work, particularly on its XLRP program (AGTC-501, now laru-zova), remains relevant for understanding advancements in gene therapy.

feed.title: South Dakota Investment Council Lowers Position in Parker-Hannifin Corporation $PH
feed.summary: The South Dakota Investment Council significantly reduced its stake in Parker-Hannifin Corporation (NYSE:PH) by 47.2% in the third quarter, selling 2,491 shares and retaining 2,791 shares valued at approximately $2.12 million. Despite this, institutional investors still hold 82.44% of the stock. Parker-Hannifin recently surpassed earnings expectations with a Q4 EPS of $7.65 and revenue of $5.17 billion, and declared a quarterly dividend of $1.80 per share.

feed.title: Jim Cramer Says Oil Sell-Off Is Green Light For New Bull Market Even As Exxon Mobil, ConocoPhillips Shares Slide
feed.summary: Jim Cramer argues that the recent decline in major energy stocks, despite Middle East tensions, signals that geopolitical risk in crude has peaked, paving the way for a stock market rally. He suggests that the oil market's predictive power, similar to the 1991 Gulf War, indicates that the price of crude has seen its peak and the market is pricing in a "defanged Iran." Cramer believes this oil sell-off is enabling a rotation into riskier assets and unleashing "bullish animal spirits," allowing the market to move past war fears into a sustained bull cycle.

feed.title: Top 2 Industrials Stocks You May Want To Dump This Month
feed.summary: As of March 5, 2026, two industrial stocks, Babcock & Wilcox (NYSE: BW) and Argan Inc (NYSE: AGX), are identified as potentially overbought based on their Relative Strength Index (RSI) values. Both companies have recently announced positive financial results and experienced significant stock gains, contributing to their high RSI scores of 78.8 for BW and 74 for AGX. Investors who use momentum as a key trading criterion might consider these stocks for close monitoring.

feed.title: Constellation Energy Stock at $323: Here’s Why Analysts See a Path to $548
feed.summary: Constellation Energy (CEG) recently completed a significant acquisition of Calpine, transforming into a diversified power provider, and reported strong Q4 adjusted EPS and revenue. Analysts have varying price targets, with a mean of $393.93 representing a 22.0% upside, while a valuation model projects a potential $547.87 target by December 2030, suggesting the market may not yet fully price in the benefits of the Calpine integration and nuclear license extensions amidst growing power demand from electrification and the data economy. The March 31 guidance call is highlighted as a critical moment for the investment thesis.

feed.title: Yum! Brands Stock After Earnings: Defensive Buy or Value Trap?
feed.summary: Yum! Brands recently updated its earnings and guidance, causing a muted stock reaction but prompting discussion on whether its growth justifies its valuation. The company, known for KFC, Taco Bell, and Pizza Hut, operates as a global, franchised cash machine that provides steady US dollar dividends. Analysts generally hold a "Moderate Buy" to "Overweight" stance, viewing it as a defensive consumer stock with stable compounding potential rather than explosive short-term growth.

feed.title: Chipotle opens first Norman location with Chipotlane drive-thru
feed.summary: Chipotle Mexican Grill has opened its first Norman location at 3170 Classen Blvd., featuring the city's inaugural Chipotlane drive-thru for digital orders. The new restaurant will also offer a college student rewards program called Chipotle U Rewards and provides employees with benefits including a bonus program, a debt-free college degree program, and mental health care access.

feed.title: FCX's Unit Cash Costs Surge in Q4: Is It Set to Climb Further in Q1?
feed.summary: Freeport-McMoRan Inc. (FCX) experienced a significant 59% surge in its average unit net cash cost per pound of copper in Q4 2025, reaching $2.22, largely due to a 29% decline in copper sales volumes following a mining incident. The company projects these costs to rise further to $2.60 per pound in Q1 2026, impacting margins. In contrast, peers Southern Copper Corporation (SCCO) and BHP Group Limited (BHP) reported lower unit costs during the same period.

feed.title: AGCO Appoints James C. Collins, Jr. to its Board of Directors
feed.summary: AGCO has announced the appointment of James C. Collins, Jr. to its Board of Directors, effective April 1, 2026. Collins brings nearly four decades of leadership experience in global agriculture, including his previous role as CEO of Corteva Agriscience. His extensive background in driving innovation and understanding the agriculture value chain aligns with AGCO's "Farmer-First" strategy.

feed.title: Four-decade agriscience leader joins AGCO board to guide smart farming push
feed.summary: AGCO (NYSE: AGCO) has appointed James C. Collins, Jr., former CEO of Corteva Agriscience, to its Board of Directors, effective April 1, 2026. Collins brings nearly four decades of leadership experience in global agriculture, innovation, and sustainable outcomes, aligning with AGCO's Farmer-First strategy. Simultaneously, director Matthew Tsien will not seek re-election, leading to a board succession change.

feed.title: DOV Boosts CO2 Refrigeration Leadership With Next Generation Flex Mini
feed.summary: Dover Corporation's subsidiary, Hillphoenix, has launched the Next Generation Flex Mini, an advanced transcritical CO2 refrigeration system. This system aims to enhance sustainability and operational performance in food retail and industrial markets by using natural refrigerants, improving energy efficiency, and reducing maintenance. The introduction solidifies Dover’s position in sustainable refrigeration technology.

feed.title: ENPHASE ENERGY, INC. (ENPH) SHAREHOLDER ALERT Bernstein
feed.summary: Bernstein Liebhard LLP has issued a shareholder alert for Enphase Energy Inc. (NASDAQ: ENPH) investors, reminding them of an upcoming deadline for a securities fraud class action lawsuit. The lawsuit alleges that Enphase made misrepresentations regarding its ability to manage channel inventory and mitigate effects from a terminated product credit. Investors who purchased shares between April 22, 2025, and October 28, 2025, are encouraged to contact the firm to discuss their legal options or file as a lead plaintiff by April 20, 2026.

feed.title: Broadcom earnings, AI's PR problem, Musk takes the stand and more in Morning Squawk
feed.summary: This Morning Squawk newsletter covers five key things investors need to know, including the Senate's vote on war powers concerning Iran, Broadcom's better-than-expected earnings driven by AI chip revenue, Elon Musk's testimony in a securities fraud lawsuit, AI's public relations challenges, and the global travel sector's difficulties amidst Middle East conflicts. Additionally, Berkshire Hathaway has resumed share repurchases for the first time since 2024.
